review: update: i've used this app for issue on the road (traffic light outage and dangerous debris on the road), and the response continues to be extremely responsive. my reports were forwarded to the department of transportation both times and resolved that day. i have used this app three times to report illegal dumping and abandoned vehicles. i live in a neighborhood where people often dump large household items on the sidewalk and prior to installing this app, i would just get annoyed with it but didn't report it. with the three cases i filed, someone came around within 24 hours to collect the dumped items or came and tagged the abandoned vehicle, really quick response you typically wouldn't expect from the city! i can understand some people's gripe with some parts of the app though--for example, if you want to report a pothole while you're driving, it's a little difficult to do so. there is no location detector, so you'll always have to manually input your location. but, if you can get an approximate address of the location, that should give the city an idea of where the problem is. overall, i've had a great experience with this app and will continue to use it!

review: i had my doubts about this when i first downloaded it and set up my account online. the company is back east, i'm in california, how will they effect change across the country? my first report was for a pothole during the rainy season and after posting it i noticed there were no city responses so i called the seeclickfix company and the man i spoke with assured me they would get my city linked up to their system. sure enough, the response came in and the pothole was fixed within days -- and that was just the first report. oh, and i was the only person in my area doing this then. a few weeks later i notice a street sign was broken - probably hit by a car - so i reported that and to my amazement, it was fixed in 3 days! this is way more effective than making phone calls. potholes are fixed within 24 hours now and my reports are now acknowledged by up to 4 city departments. i recently reported illegal commercial signage, specifically, a window washer company nailed signs on telephone posts in several places in the neighborhood and again, the response was immediate. i love this app and highly recommend it - i've never had any problems with bugs in this app.

review: i love this app! works well and is very convenient. i found the tips on zooming into the map to a specific address and to be as descriptive as possible very helpful. i reported several potholes with some pictures with mostly successful responses. the city fixed the issues within the week, and others were acknowledged, but has yet-to-be-fixed/ignored. although i always fill in the description blanks, the app does not necessarily submit the information. and you do not notice this until sometime after you have submitted your issue. for photo submissions, i did find it easier to take your photos prior to using the app (which you then search and select from your photo gallery). once you have submitted your issue, you are emailed an notification of your issue, which you can check on its status. one star off for some description and picture improvements to be made. note: gta-toronto location.
